description: Program 2 baseline hypotheses. These detectors test whether off-line / perturbed zeros produce structured signatures under compression. Their failure first scopes to baseline-or-route, never automatically to Program 1 or to the base claim.

hypotheses.plain_statement: A planted rogue / off-line zero produces detectable amplification under the tested compression and gauge settings.

expected_signature.pass_rule: amplification exceeds threshold for every tested perturbation

hypotheses.why_this_matters: If a planted rogue cannot be detected, NC6 (no hiding under compression) is threatened — but only with respect to the chosen detection metric. The Program 2 contradiction route depends on detectability.
failure_means: the current detectability baseline or chosen metric may be wrong or incomplete
Program 2 detection coverage in this window is weak
the perturbation amplitude may be below the instrument floor
possible_alternative_hypotheses: detectability requires a different compression depth
detectability requires phase-aware metrics rather than amplitude metrics
detectability holds only for perturbations above a structured floor
intended_inference_if_passed: structured rogue zeros produce a detectable signature on the tested window
the Program 2 contradiction route remains armed in this window
this is not a proof of NC6

hypotheses.plain_statement: A single-perturbed-zero model predicts residual behavior according to the current residual-ratio model (observed/predicted residual ~ 1.0 under the tested envelope).

expected_signature.pass_rule: ratio remains near 1.0 across the tested k / window range

hypotheses.why_this_matters: Residual isolation is the finite-precision baseline for NC6 via single-perturbation modeling. The current ratio model is one specific baseline among several plausible models.
failure_means: the current residual-ratio baseline was not confirmed
the single-perturbation model may be incomplete
the chosen normalization or window may be wrong
possible_alternative_hypotheses: k-depth dependent attenuation of the residual signal
multi-zero interference dominates over single-perturbation contribution
wrong normalization of the observed/predicted ratio
hypotheses.candidate_lemma_name: Residual Isolation / Residual Envelope Candidate Lemma
intended_inference_if_passed: the single-perturbation residual model is consistent on the tested window
the Program 2 detection route via residual ratios remains armed
this is not a proof of NC6
disallowed_conclusions: this is consistent with Program 1 in any direct sense

hypotheses.plain_statement: Rogue amplification behaves monotonically or in a structured way across the epsilon perturbation sweep.

expected_signature.expected_value: monotonic or otherwise structurally predicted curve
expected_signature.pass_rule: the amplification curve fits the predicted structural form

hypotheses.why_this_matters: Calibrated amplification is the metrology baseline for Program 2: it gauges whether the detection instrument tracks perturbation size in a predictable way. Without calibration, NC6 cannot be quantified.
failure_means: the current amplification model is incomplete
the calibration baseline is not confirmed in this window
possible_alternative_hypotheses: amplification curve has structured non-monotonicity in some regime
calibration is regime-split between small and large epsilon
amplification depends on phase of the planted perturbation
intended_inference_if_passed: Program 2 metrology is consistent on the tested epsilon range
the contradiction-track instrument is calibrated for this window
this is not a proof of NC6
